name: taskweft
description: Homoiconic RECTGTN planner: accepts a JSON-LD domain or problem, plans/simulates/replans, and returns a JSON-LD plan. Input and output share the same structure — a plan IS a domain where tasks are the resolved action sequence and variables reflect final state. The output can be fed back as input for replan, simulate, or further composition. Supports all 8 built-in domains (simple_travel, blocks_world, rescue, robosub, healthcare, temporal_travel, entity_capabilities, job_shop_scheduling) via preset, or accepts a full custom domain inline. Use mode='plan' for initial planning, mode='simulate' to step through a plan, mode='replan' to recover from a failed action.

mode.description: Operation mode. plan: generate a plan from domain+problem. simulate: step through an existing plan and return state transitions. replan: recover from a failed action at fail_step index.

preset.description: Built-in domain name. Loads the domain JSON-LD from the domains directory. Omit if providing a full domain inline.

problem.description: Built-in problem name (e.g. 'simple_travel_two', 'blocks_world_1b'). Loads problem JSON-LD which overrides variables, tasks, and optionally methods from the preset domain.

domain.description: Full JSON-LD domain definition inline. Overrides preset. Must include @context, @type, name, variables, actions, methods, and tasks.

variables.description: Override initial state variables. Each item: {name, init: {key: value, ...}}.

tasks.description: Override task list. Each item is [task_name, arg1, ...] or {multigoal: {var: {key: val}}}.

methods.description: Additional method definitions to merge into the domain (e.g. move_blocks with scan).

plan_jsonld.description: For simulate/replan: a previously returned plan_jsonld object. Contains the domain actions, resolved plan tasks, and final state.

fail_step.description: For replan: the 0-based index of the action that failed during execution.

state.description: For replan: the observed state at the point of failure, as a dict of {var_name: {key: val}}.
